title: Palo Alto Networks Security Advisory
description: Schema for a Palo Alto Networks PSIRT (Product Security Incident Response Team) security advisory. Security advisories disclose vulnerabilities affecting Palo Alto Networks products including PAN-OS, Cortex XDR, Prisma Cloud, Prisma Access, GlobalProtect, and other solutions. Each advisory provides vulnerability details, CVSS scoring, affected product versions, fixed versions, workarounds, and references aligned with CVE and CVSS standards. Advisories are published on the Palo Alto Networks Security Advisories page and updated as new information becomes available.

advisoryId.description: Unique identifier for the security advisory assigned by the Palo Alto Networks PSIRT. Follows the format 'PAN-SA-YYYY-NNNN' for security advisories or 'PAN-IN-YYYY-NNNN' for informational bulletins.

cveId.description: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) identifier assigned to the vulnerability by a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA). Palo Alto Networks serves as a CNA for vulnerabilities in its own products. Format is 'CVE-' followed by a four-digit year and a sequence number of four or more digits.

title.description: Brief descriptive title of the security advisory summarizing the vulnerability type and affected product component. Should clearly communicate the nature of the vulnerability (e.g., 'PAN-OS: OS Command Injection Vulnerability in Management Interface').

severity.description: Qualitative severity rating of the vulnerability based on the CVSS base score range. 'none' indicates a CVSS score of 0.0, 'low' maps to 0.1-3.9, 'medium' maps to 4.0-6.9, 'high' maps to 7.0-8.9, and 'critical' maps to 9.0-10.0. The severity rating determines the urgency of patch deployment.

cvssBaseScore.description: Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) version 3.1 base score representing the intrinsic severity of the vulnerability. The score ranges from 0.0 (no impact) to 10.0 (maximum severity) and is calculated from the attack vector, complexity, privileges required, user interaction, scope, and impact metrics.

cvssVector.description: CVSS version 3.1 vector string encoding the individual metrics used to calculate the base score. Format follows the CVSS v3.1 specification (e.g., 'CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H'). Each metric abbreviation represents an attack characteristic.

description.description: Detailed technical description of the vulnerability including the root cause, attack prerequisites, and exploitation mechanism. Should explain what the vulnerability is, where it exists in the product, and under what conditions it can be exploited. Supports markdown formatting.

impact.description: Description of the potential security impact if the vulnerability is successfully exploited. Should describe the consequences in terms of confidentiality, integrity, and availability impacts, as well as any potential for lateral movement, privilege escalation, or data exfiltration.

exploitStatus.description: Current known exploitation status of the vulnerability. 'none' indicates no known exploitation or proof-of-concept code, 'poc' indicates that proof-of-concept exploit code exists publicly or has been demonstrated, and 'active' indicates that the vulnerability is being actively exploited in the wild by threat actors.

affectedProducts.description: List of Palo Alto Networks products and versions affected by the vulnerability. Each entry identifies a specific product, the vulnerable version ranges, and optionally the hardware or virtual platforms affected.

fixedVersions.description: List of product versions that contain the fix for the vulnerability. Each entry identifies the specific product, version number, and release date of the patched software. Customers should upgrade to one of these versions or later to remediate the vulnerability.

workarounds.description: Description of temporary mitigations or configuration changes that can reduce the risk of exploitation before a patched version is deployed. Workarounds may include restricting network access, disabling affected features, applying configuration hardening, or using Threat Prevention signatures. Supports markdown formatting.

acknowledgments.description: List of individuals, organizations, or teams who discovered or reported the vulnerability to Palo Alto Networks PSIRT. Acknowledgments recognize responsible disclosure efforts by external security researchers, partner organizations, or internal teams.

items.description: Name and optional organizational affiliation of the reporter (e.g., 'John Smith of Acme Security Research').

publishedDate.description: Date when the security advisory was first publicly published on the Palo Alto Networks Security Advisories page. Formatted as an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD).

lastUpdatedDate.description: Date when the security advisory was last updated with new information such as additional affected products, fixed versions, workaround changes, or exploitation status updates. Formatted as an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD).

references.description: List of external references providing additional information about the vulnerability, including CVE entries, vendor advisories, CERT/CC advisories, threat intelligence reports, and related documentation.

threatPreventionSignatures.description: List of Palo Alto Networks Threat Prevention signature identifiers that detect or block exploitation of the vulnerability. These signatures can be deployed on PAN-OS firewalls and Prisma Access to provide network-level protection.

AffectedProduct.description: Describes a specific Palo Alto Networks product and the version ranges affected by the vulnerability. Products include PAN-OS (firewall operating system), Cortex XDR, Cortex XSOAR, Prisma Cloud, Prisma Access, GlobalProtect, and other security solutions.

product.description: Name of the affected Palo Alto Networks product (e.g., 'PAN-OS', 'Cortex XDR Agent', 'Cortex XSOAR', 'Prisma Cloud Compute', 'Prisma Access', 'GlobalProtect App', 'Cloud NGFW').

versions.description: List of affected version ranges or specific version identifiers. May include version ranges using comparison operators (e.g., '< 10.2.5', '>= 10.1.0'), specific versions (e.g., '10.2.3'), or branch identifiers (e.g., '10.2', '11.0').

items.description: A version string, version range, or version branch identifier.

platforms.description: List of hardware or virtual platforms within the product family that are affected. When not specified, all platforms for the product are considered affected. Examples include specific firewall models (PA-220, PA-3200 Series), virtual form factors (VM-Series, CN-Series), or deployment types (Cloud NGFW for AWS).

defaultStatus.description: Default vulnerability status for versions not explicitly listed. Indicates whether unlisted versions should be assumed affected or unaffected.

FixedVersion.description: Identifies a specific product version that includes the vulnerability fix. Customers should upgrade to this version or any later maintenance release within the same branch to remediate the vulnerability.

product.description: Name of the Palo Alto Networks product for which the fixed version is available.

version.description: Specific software version number that contains the vulnerability fix (e.g., '10.2.5', '11.0.2-h1', '3.1.4'). Hotfix versions are indicated with the '-hN' suffix.

releaseDate.description: Date when the fixed version was released or is expected to be released. Formatted as an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Future dates indicate planned releases that may be subject to change.

Reference.description: An external reference providing additional context or information about the vulnerability. References typically include CVE database entries, related vendor advisories, CERT/CC vulnerability notes, and threat intelligence reports.

url.description: URL of the external reference resource. Must be a fully qualified URI including the protocol scheme.

description.description: Brief description of the reference resource, explaining what additional information it provides about the vulnerability (e.g., 'NIST NVD Entry', 'MITRE CVE Record', 'CERT/CC Vulnerability Note').

ThreatPreventionSignature.description: A Palo Alto Networks Threat Prevention signature that provides network-level detection and blocking capability for exploitation of the vulnerability. Signatures are delivered through Threat Prevention content updates.

signatureId.description: Unique numeric identifier for the Threat Prevention signature. Used to reference the signature in PAN-OS security profiles and threat logs.

signatureName.description: Descriptive name of the Threat Prevention signature, typically including the CVE identifier and vulnerability description.

minimumContentVersion.description: Minimum Applications and Threats content update version required to include this signature. Format is 'NNNN-NNNN' (e.g., '8765-8321').
